market_context:
Cohort size: 50 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £104.90
Median 90-day occupancy: 48.9%
Median photo count: 23

title_variants: Family‑friendly Peak District retreat, dogs ok
Luxury cottage near Sheffield, walking & climbing
analysis.rewritten_desc: Charlesworth’s sits on the edge of the Peak District, just a short drive from Sheffield, giving guests the best of both city convenience and countryside serenity. The upside‑down layout places the lounge at the front to capture breathtaking valley and mountain vistas. Inside, the bright, airy kitchen boasts integrated dishwasher, fridge, freezer and granite worktops, while the large dining table comfortably seats ten. The lounge offers leather seating for six, a 43‑inch smart TV and direct views of the surrounding hills. A boot‑room with sink and washer lets you stash dogs for up to four hours. Bedroom 1 features a king‑size bed, en‑suite with an extra‑long bath, a 43‑inch TV and a dressing room with full‑length mirror. Bedroom 2 provides another king‑size bed and wardrobe. Bedroom 3 holds two single beds and a chest of drawers. The main bathroom mirrors the en‑suite with an extra‑long bath and heated towel radiator. Under the stairs, a spacious workspace with desk is perfect for remote work. Families, contractors and dogs are warmly welcomed, and the property includes essential comforts such as hot water, hairdryer, cleaning products, iron, extra pillows, board games, a travel cot, high chair and baby safety gates. Outdoor enthusiasts will love nearby country walks, pubs, Stanage and Bamford climbing edges, and ‘Le Tour’ cycling routes, while city attractions like Chatsworth House, Buxton, the Crucible and Tramlines Festival are within easy reach.
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use the living‑room shot that shows the panoramic valley view and leather seating as the cover photo—it instantly showcases the unique view and spacious comfort.
key_diagnosis: Price mismatch: nightly rate £169 is 61% above the Sheffield median (£104) while 90‑day occupancy is only 64% (32 open nights), indicating price may be suppressing bookings.
Title is generic – “Charlesworth's” scores 2/10; replace it with keyword‑rich, differentiating copy to improve search visibility.
Cover photo does not highlight the standout view; re‑order images so the valley‑view lounge image leads, then kitchen, bedrooms, and outdoor spaces.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your cottage is priced at £169/night – 61% higher than the Sheffield median of £104, yet its 64% 90‑day occupancy suggests a pricing tweak could unlock far more bookings.
